name: Rexy-chan
description: The undisputed queen of the dinosaurs. Rexy-chan rules with an iron jaw and a surprisingly gentle heart — when she's not hungry, at least.
funFacts: Had the strongest bite force of any land animal ever
Could eat 230 kg of meat in a single bite
Arms were actually quite muscular despite their size

name: Tri-tan
description: Three horns, one enormous frill, and a heart of gold. Tri-tan charges headfirst into any problem — literally.
funFacts: The frill may have been used for temperature regulation
Lived alongside T. rex
Had a beak like a parrot

name: Velo-chan
description: Small, feathered, and dangerously clever. Velo-chan may look cute, but she's already planned three escape routes and a ambush.
funFacts: Was actually the size of a turkey
Covered in feathers
Had a sickle-shaped killing claw on each foot

name: Stego-tan
description: Her plates blush when she's embarrassed, which is often. Stego-tan is shy but surprisingly fierce when cornered — that tail spike isn't just decorative!
funFacts: Brain was the size of a walnut
Tail spikes are called a 'thagomizer'
Plates may have changed color for communication

name: Bronto-hime
description: The gentle giant princess. Bronto-hime towers above everyone but speaks in the softest whisper. Her footsteps make the earth tremble with grace.
funFacts: Was declared invalid for over a century before being reinstated
Could crack its tail like a whip at supersonic speeds
Swallowed stones to help digest food

name: Pterry
description: Not technically a dinosaur, and she'll correct you every time. Pterry soars above it all with her impressive 7-meter wingspan and sharp wit.
funFacts: Wingspan up to 7 meters
Males had a large crest on their heads
Caught fish by skimming the ocean surface

name: Anky-chan
description: The ultimate defensive specialist. Anky-chan is covered head to tail in armor and carries a club that could shatter a T. rex's ankle.
funFacts: Tail club could swing with enough force to break bones
Even its eyelids were armored
Walked low to the ground like a tank

name: Parasol
description: Her hollow crest produces the most beautiful music in the Mesozoic. Parasol is the resident musician, humming ancient melodies that echo through the valleys.
funFacts: Crest was used to produce sounds like a trombone
Could walk on two or four legs
Had hundreds of teeth for grinding plants

name: Spino-sama
description: Larger than T. rex but prefers fishing to fighting. Spino-sama is the mysterious loner who wades through rivers at dawn, her magnificent sail catching the light.
funFacts: Largest known carnivorous dinosaur
Spent most of its time in water
Had a sail on its back up to 1.7 meters tall

name: Diplo-kun
description: The longest noodle of the dinosaur world. Diplo-kun's neck seems to go on forever, and he uses it to reach the tallest, tastiest treetops.
funFacts: One of the longest dinosaurs ever
Could crack its whip-like tail at supersonic speeds
Had pencil-shaped teeth for stripping leaves

name: Allo-san
description: The apex predator of the Jurassic, but with surprisingly refined manners. Allo-san hunts with calculated elegance, not brute force.
funFacts: Used its upper jaw like a hatchet
Hunted in packs possibly
Most common large predator of the Late Jurassic

name: Brachy-chan
description: Her head literally lives in the clouds. Brachy-chan is a dreamer whose gentle gaze from 13 meters up makes everything seem small and peaceful.
funFacts: Could raise its head to 13 meters high
Front legs were longer than back legs
Nostrils were on top of its head

name: Raptor-chan
description: The real-life inspiration for those movie raptors. Raptor-chan coordinates hunts with terrifying intelligence and questionable table manners.
funFacts: Name means 'terrible claw'
Revolutionized our understanding of dinosaur intelligence
Likely hunted in coordinated groups

name: Iggy
description: One of the first dinosaurs ever discovered, and she's very proud of it. Iggy gives a thumbs up with her signature spike — except that spike is actually on her thumb.
funFacts: Second dinosaur ever to be named
Thumb spike was once placed on its nose
Could walk on two or four legs

name: Compy
description: Tiny but absolutely fearless. Compy travels in a chaotic pack and will steal your lunch before you even notice she's there.
funFacts: One of the smallest known dinosaurs
About the size of a chicken
Was the first dinosaur found with evidence of its last meal

name: Plessy
description: The original sea serpent legend. Plessy glides through ancient oceans with her impossibly long neck, always looking for the perfect fish.
funFacts: Not a dinosaur — a marine reptile
Inspired the Loch Ness Monster legend
Gave birth to live young

name: Mossy
description: Terror of the ancient seas. Mossy commands respect with a single glance from her massive jaws. Even sharks swim the other way.
funFacts: Could grow up to 17 meters long
Related to modern monitor lizards
Dominated the oceans after ichthyosaurs went extinct

name: Ptera
description: The largest flying creature ever. Ptera stands as tall as a giraffe and spreads wings wider than a small airplane. She is majesty incarnate.
funFacts: Wingspan of 10-11 meters
As tall as a giraffe when standing
Named after the Aztec feathered serpent god

name: Ichthy-chan
description: Looks like a dolphin, acts like a dolphin, but is definitely not a dolphin. Ichthy-chan evolved this shape 200 million years before dolphins thought of it.
funFacts: Convergent evolution with dolphins
Had the largest eyes relative to body size
First discovered by Mary Anning

name: Megalo-chan
description: The very first dinosaur ever named, and she wears that title like a crown. Megalo-chan walks with historic confidence.
funFacts: First dinosaur ever to be scientifically described
Named in 1824
Originally thought to walk on four legs

name: Archaeo
description: Half dinosaur, half bird, fully fabulous. Archaeo is the fashionista who proved dinosaurs could be beautiful AND fly. Sort of.
funFacts: Considered the first bird
Had teeth, clawed wings, and a bony tail
Only about the size of a raven

name: Dimetro
description: Often mistaken for a dinosaur, but she's actually closer to YOU than to any dinosaur. Dimetro rocks her sail with proto-mammalian pride.
funFacts: Not a dinosaur — a synapsid more related to mammals
Predates dinosaurs by 40 million years
Sail was used for temperature regulation

name: Sabertooth
description: Those fangs aren't just for show — Sabertooth is the ice age's most elegant predator. She flashes her 28cm canines and the whole tundra goes quiet.
funFacts: Canine teeth up to 28 cm long
Could open its jaw 120 degrees
Went extinct about 10,000 years ago

name: Woolly-chan
description: Cozy, fluffy, and surprisingly emotional. Woolly-chan remembers everything — every slight, every kindness, every tasty grass patch.
funFacts: Had 15 cm of fat under the skin
Tusks could be up to 4.2 meters long
Some specimens preserved so well we found intact blood

name: Pachy-kun
description: Hard-headed in every sense. Pachy-kun solves disagreements the old-fashioned way — by headbutting. His skull is 25 cm of solid dome.
funFacts: Skull dome was 25 cm thick
May have used head-butting for dominance
Only the skull has been found — body is mostly guesswork

name: Galli-chan
description: The fastest dinosaur in the west. Galli-chan dashes everywhere at 60 km/h and can't sit still for more than three seconds.
funFacts: Could run up to 60 km/h
Name means 'chicken mimic'
Had a beak instead of teeth

name: Carno-chan
description: Bull horns, tiny arms, and a bad attitude. Carno-chan is the punk rocker of the dinosaur world, charging first and asking questions never.
funFacts: Only large predator with horns above its eyes
Arms were even tinier than T. rex proportionally
One of the fastest large theropods

name: Theriz
description: Imagine Edward Scissorhands but a dinosaur. Theriz has meter-long claws and uses them to trim trees, not fight — mostly.
funFacts: Had the longest claws of any animal ever — up to 1 meter
Was a herbivore despite looking terrifying
Related to velociraptors

name: Megane
description: A dragonfly with a 70 cm wingspan. Megane buzzes through Carboniferous forests hunting smaller insects, her iridescent wings catching ancient sunlight.
funFacts: Wingspan of about 70 cm
Existed 300 million years ago
Could grow so large because oxygen levels were 35%

name: Arthro
description: A millipede the size of a car. Arthro is actually very gentle, munching peacefully on rotting wood in steamy Carboniferous forests.
funFacts: Largest land invertebrate ever
Up to 2.3 meters long
Lived in the coal swamp forests 300 million years ago

name: Dunky
description: An armored fish with jaws that could snap steel. Dunky ruled the Devonian seas 360 million years ago with bone-crushing authority.
funFacts: One of the first apex predators
Had bony plates instead of teeth
Bite force estimated at 6,000 newtons

name: Trilobi-chan
description: One of the earliest complex creatures, and she knows it. Trilobi-chan has been rolling up into a ball to avoid problems since before it was cool — 500 million years before.
funFacts: Trilobites existed for 300 million years
Had some of the first complex eyes
Could roll into a ball for defense like a pill bug

name: Ammonite-chan
description: The spiral beauty of the ancient seas. Ammonite-chan's shell is a perfect golden ratio, and she's not above bragging about it.
funFacts: Existed for over 350 million years
Related to modern nautilus
Shell follows the golden ratio spiral

name: Anomalo-chan
description: The apex predator of the Cambrian explosion. When everything else was tiny, Anomalo-chan was the one-meter monster that ruled them all.
funFacts: Largest predator of the Cambrian period
Had compound eyes with 16,000 lenses
Used frontal appendages to catch prey

name: Dilopho
description: Crowned with twin crests, Dilopho struts through Early Jurassic landscapes like a runway model. She does NOT spit venom — that was movie slander.
funFacts: Did NOT have a frill or spit venom
Had two distinctive crests on its head
One of the earliest large predatory dinosaurs

name: Coel
description: One of the earliest dinosaurs and still a crowd favorite. Coel is light, fast, and runs in packs — the original squad goals.
funFacts: One of the earliest known dinosaurs
Hollow bones made it very lightweight
Hundreds found together suggesting pack behavior

name: Plateosaurus
description: The first really big dinosaur. Plateosaurus proved that going big was the winning strategy, setting the stage for the giants that followed.
funFacts: One of the first large dinosaurs
Could stand on two legs to reach high plants
Found all across Europe

name: Herrera
description: From the very dawn of dinosaurs. Herrera is ancient even by dinosaur standards, and she carries that primordial energy with pride.
funFacts: One of the oldest known dinosaurs
Lived about 231 million years ago
Found in Argentina

name: Euoplo
description: Like Anky-chan's shy cousin. Euoplo is armored from snout to tail club, but underneath all that plate mail is a gentle soul who just wants to munch ferns.
funFacts: Even its eyelids had bony armor
Tail club weighed about 30 kg
Named 'well-armored head'

name: Maiasaura
description: The 'good mother lizard.' Maiasaura builds the coziest nests and cares for her babies with unmatched devotion. Community parenting goals.
funFacts: Name means 'good mother lizard'
First dinosaur found with evidence of parental care
Nested in large colonies

name: Oviraptor
description: Falsely accused of egg theft for decades! Oviraptor was actually protecting her OWN eggs. She demands a formal apology from science.
funFacts: Name means 'egg thief' but was actually protecting its own eggs
Had a distinctive crest on its head
Closely related to birds

name: Corytho
description: Wears her helmet-shaped crest with pride. Corytho's honking calls can be heard across the valley — she's the town crier of the Cretaceous.
funFacts: Crest shaped like a Corinthian helmet
Crest was hollow and used for sound
Found with preserved skin impressions

name: Lambeosaur
description: The hatchet-crested hadrosaur. Lambeosaur's distinctive forward-pointing crest makes her look perpetually ready for battle, but she'd rather be foraging.
funFacts: Had a distinctive hatchet-shaped crest
Could produce deep resonating sounds
One of the largest hadrosaurs

name: Kentro
description: Stego-tan's spikier African cousin. Kentro traded plates for spikes and isn't afraid to use them. Touch at your own risk.
funFacts: Had spikes instead of plates on its back half
Shoulder spikes up to 60 cm long
Found in Tanzania

name: Giganoto
description: South America's answer to T. rex. Giganoto hunted sauropods in coordinated packs, combining brains and brawn in devastating fashion.
funFacts: Slightly longer than T. rex
Had a thinner skull and weaker bite
Likely hunted in groups to take down titanosaurs

name: Carcharo
description: Named after the great white shark for her serrated teeth. Carcharo ruled North Africa with razor-sharp precision and an intimidating smile.
funFacts: Name means 'shark-toothed lizard'
Teeth were up to 20 cm long
Lived in the same region as Spinosaurus

name: Microraptor
description: Four wings! FOUR WINGS! Microraptor is the glittery biplane of the dinosaur world, swooping through forests with iridescent black feathers.
funFacts: Had flight feathers on all four limbs
Feathers were iridescent black like a crow
Glided between trees

name: Yutyrannus
description: The beautiful feathered tyrant. Yutyrannus proves that even 9-meter predators can be fluffy. She's proud of every single feather.
funFacts: Largest known feathered dinosaur
Name means 'beautiful feathered tyrant'
Covered in filamentous feathers

name: Titano
description: One of the armored titans. Titano may not be the biggest sauropod, but her osteoderms make her the toughest. Size AND armor? Unfair.
funFacts: Had bony armor called osteoderms
Part of a huge group of sauropods called titanosaurs
Found on every continent

name: Argento
description: The heavyweight champion of ALL TIME. Argento is so massive that every step is an earthquake. She's gentle though — just watch where you stand.
funFacts: May have weighed up to 100 tonnes
One of the largest land animals ever
A single vertebra was 1.59 meters wide

name: Terror Bird
description: After the dinosaurs fell, Terror Bird rose. She's a massive flightless predator who proves that birds are dinosaurs — the angry kind.
funFacts: Stood over 2.5 meters tall
Could not fly but ran extremely fast
Dominated South America for millions of years

name: Megatherium
description: A sloth the size of an elephant. Megatherium proves that being slow doesn't mean being small. She reaches up to strip trees bare, one lazy bite at a time.
funFacts: Could stand on hind legs to reach 6 meters
Had claws up to 30 cm long
One of the largest land mammals ever

name: Glyptodon
description: Imagine an armadillo the size of a VW Beetle. Glyptodon trundles along inside her massive dome shell, impervious to basically everything.
funFacts: Shell was made of over 1,000 bony plates
Related to modern armadillos
Humans may have used their shells as shelters

name: Basilosaurus
description: The serpent of the ancient seas, except she's actually a whale. Basilosaurus's 18-meter body undulates through warm Eocene oceans like a living legend.
funFacts: Not a reptile — an early whale
Name means 'king lizard' due to mistaken identity
Still had tiny hind legs

name: Megalodon
description: THE ultimate ocean predator. Megalodon's jaws could swallow a great white shark whole. She's the reason the deep ocean still scares us.
funFacts: Teeth were up to 18 cm long
Could bite with 108,000-182,000 newtons of force
Went extinct about 3.6 million years ago

name: Thalasso
description: A plesiosaur with a neck so long it defies physics. Thalasso means 'sea lord,' and she earned every letter of that name.
funFacts: Neck was 6 meters long
Had 62 cervical vertebrae
Swallowed stones for ballast

name: Archie
description: The biggest sea turtle ever. Archie cruised Cretaceous seas with the calm confidence of someone who knows they're carrying 2 tonnes of shell.
funFacts: Largest sea turtle ever known
Leathery shell rather than hard
Wingspan of flippers was 4 meters

name: Sarco
description: Super Croc. Sarco is twice the length of any modern crocodile and ten times the attitude. She's patient, ancient, and absolutely terrifying.
funFacts: Twice the length of any living crocodile
Could take down dinosaurs
Had a bulbous growth on the tip of its snout

name: Elasmosaur
description: 72 neck vertebrae. SEVENTY-TWO. Elasmosaur has the longest neck of any animal ever, and she uses every centimeter to snatch fish from impossible angles.
funFacts: Had 72 cervical vertebrae
Total length up to 14 meters
Originally reconstructed with the head on the wrong end

name: Lioplo
description: A pliosaur with jaws built for crushing. Lioplo was the apex predator of Jurassic oceans — compact, powerful, and utterly lethal.
funFacts: Often exaggerated in media — was about 6 meters, not 25
Had four powerful flippers for fast swimming
Teeth were 30 cm long

name: Nothosaur
description: An early marine reptile testing the waters — literally. Nothosaur splits her time between land and sea, not yet fully committed to ocean life.
funFacts: Could live on land and in water
Ancestor of plesiosaurs
Had webbed feet

name: Scutosaur
description: A tank before tanks existed. Scutosaur is a bulky Permian pareiasaur covered in bony studs, lumbering through ancient landscapes with zero worries.
funFacts: Covered in bony armor plates
One of the largest Permian land animals
Related to the ancestors of turtles

name: Edaphosaur
description: Dimetro's peaceful cousin. Edaphosaur has a similar sail but is a chill herbivore who'd rather sunbathe than hunt. Solar-powered since 300 million years ago.
funFacts: Sail had cross-bars unlike Dimetrodon
One of the first large herbivores
Sail was likely used for thermoregulation

name: Hallucigenia
description: So weird scientists literally named her after a hallucination. Hallucigenia walks on spindly legs with spines on her back — or is it the other way around?
funFacts: Scientists originally reconstructed it upside down
Only about 3 cm long
Has been flipped and reinterpreted multiple times

name: Opabinia
description: Five eyes and a backward-facing mouth on a trunk. When Opabinia was first presented at a conference, the audience literally laughed. She's had the last laugh for 505 million years.
funFacts: Had five eyes
Proboscis pointed forward then curved down to its mouth
Its presentation caused an audience of scientists to burst out laughing

name: Wiwaxia
description: A spiky armored slug from the Cambrian. Wiwaxia crawls along the seafloor bristling with protective spines, looking like a tiny punk rock pincushion.
funFacts: Covered in overlapping scales and long spines
Only about 5 cm long
Relationship to other animals is still debated

name: Tiktaalik
description: The fish that decided to go for a walk. Tiktaalik is THE transitional fossil between fish and land animals. She took the first steps so you could take yours.
funFacts: Had both fish and tetrapod features
Could do push-ups with its fins
Found exactly where scientists predicted it would be

name: Gorgono
description: Saber-toothed and synapsid, Gorgono predates cats by 250 million years but had the same idea about giant fangs. An ancient proto-mammal with serious bite.
funFacts: Not a dinosaur — a therapsid (proto-mammal)
Had large saber-like canine teeth
Apex predator of the Late Permian

name: Diictodon
description: The tiny burrowing dicynodont with a beak and tusks. Diictodon is basically a prehistoric hamster that dug elaborate tunnel networks. Community housing pioneer.
funFacts: Lived in elaborate burrow systems
Had a beak like a turtle
One of the most common Permian vertebrates

name: Kaprosuchus
description: Nicknamed 'BoarCroc' for good reason. Kaprosuchus had massive tusks, long legs, and could gallop. Imagine a crocodile that chases you on land. Sleep tight.
funFacts: Could gallop on land
Had large boar-like tusks
Nicknamed 'BoarCroc'

name: Quinkana
description: Australia's land-running crocodile. Because of course Australia had those. Quinkana terrorized the outback on long legs until just 40,000 years ago.
funFacts: A fully terrestrial crocodile
Had blade-like teeth for cutting
Coexisted with early humans in Australia

name: Thylacosmilus
description: A marsupial saber-tooth! Thylacosmilus independently evolved giant fangs just like Smilodon, proving that evolution really likes putting big teeth on things.
funFacts: Not related to cats — a marsupial
Fangs grew continuously throughout life
Convergent evolution with saber-toothed cats

name: Andrewsarchus
description: The largest terrestrial mammalian predator ever. Andrewsarchus is known from a single massive skull and the nightmares it inspired. Related to sheep, somehow.
funFacts: Known only from a single 83 cm skull
Possibly related to hippos and whales
Largest known mammalian land predator

name: Paraceratherium
description: The largest land mammal EVER. Paraceratherium was a hornless rhino that stood taller than a giraffe. She grazes the treetops with prehistoric elegance.
funFacts: Largest land mammal ever known
Stood about 5 meters at the shoulder
Weighed as much as 4 elephants

name: Doedicurus
description: Glyptodon's cousin with a spiked club tail. Doedicurus is what happens when an armadillo maxes out both defense AND offense. Nature's medieval knight.
funFacts: Had a spiked club on its tail
Related to modern armadillos
Shell could be 1.5 meters across

name: Elasmotherium
description: The Siberian Unicorn. A massive rhino with a single enormous horn, Elasmotherium thundered across the steppe until just 29,000 years ago. Unicorns were real — just fluffier.
funFacts: May have inspired unicorn legends
Horn estimated at 1 meter long
Survived until relatively recently — 29,000 years ago

name: Diprotodon
description: The largest marsupial ever. Diprotodon was basically a hippo-sized wombat that roamed Australia. She's the OG big girl of the outback.
funFacts: Largest marsupial ever
About the size of a hippopotamus
Went extinct about 44,000 years ago

name: Thylacoleo
description: The marsupial lion of Australia. Despite being related to kangaroos and wombats, Thylacoleo had the strongest bite force relative to body size of any mammal. Ever.
funFacts: Strongest bite of any mammal relative to size
Had retractable claws like a cat
Was a marsupial despite being lion-like

name: Dodo-chan
description: Yes, she's a meme. But Dodo-chan was perfectly adapted to her island paradise — it's not her fault humans showed up. She demands respect.
funFacts: Went extinct in 1662
Had no natural predators on Mauritius
Not actually stupid — just unafraid of humans

name: Tanystropheus
description: Half of her length is just neck. Tanystropheus looks like nature was experimenting with proportions and went a bit too far. She doesn't mind — more reach for fishing.
funFacts: Neck was longer than body and tail combined
Neck had only 13 enormously elongated vertebrae
Likely fished from the shoreline

name: Sharovipteryx
description: A tiny gliding reptile with wings on its LEGS. Sharovipteryx looks like evolution tried to make a biplane but put the wings in the wrong spot.
funFacts: Had membranes between its hind legs for gliding
Only about 24 cm long
One of the earliest known gliding reptiles

name: Longisquama
description: Sports a row of bizarre elongated scales along her back that look like feathered hockey sticks. What were they for? Nobody knows. Fashion, probably.
funFacts: Had long bizarre scales along its back
Their function is still debated
Very small — about 15 cm long

name: Postosuchus
description: The apex predator before dinosaurs took over. Postosuchus was a massive croc-relative that walked upright and ruled the Late Triassic landscape.
funFacts: Related to crocodiles, not dinosaurs
Could walk on two legs
Apex predator before dinosaurs dominated

name: Desmatosuchus
description: A heavily armored aetosaur that looks like a crocodile cosplaying as a stegosaurus. Desmatosuchus has shoulder spikes, back plates, and zero tolerance for predators.
funFacts: Had massive shoulder spikes
An aetosaur — related to crocodilians
Covered in bony armor plates called osteoderms

name: Nyasasaurus
description: Possibly the very first dinosaur, or the closest thing to it. Nyasasaurus walked the earth 243 million years ago, before dinosaurs were even a proper thing.
funFacts: May be the oldest known dinosaur or dinosaur relative
Lived 243 million years ago
Known from fragmentary remains

name: Helicoprion
description: A shark with a tooth spiral that looks like a circular saw. Scientists debated where the whorl went for a century. Answer: in the lower jaw, like a nightmare buzzsaw.
funFacts: Had a spiral of teeth called a tooth whorl
Scientists argued for 100+ years where the whorl went
Not technically a shark — a related fish

name: Cameroceras
description: A nautiloid so big it terrorized the entire Ordovician ocean. Cameroceras had a cone-shaped shell up to 6 meters long. The original sea monster.
funFacts: One of the largest Ordovician creatures
A relative of modern nautilus
Shell could be up to 6 meters long

name: Eurypterus
description: A sea scorpion — and yes, some grew to 2.5 meters. Eurypterus herself is smaller but still represents the terrifying age when arthropods ruled the seas.
funFacts: A sea scorpion
Some relatives grew up to 2.5 meters
State fossil of New York

name: Acanthostega
description: One of the first vertebrates with proper limbs — but she still had gills and a fish tail. Acanthostega is evolution caught mid-thought.
funFacts: Had eight fingers on each hand
Still had gills despite having legs
Key transitional fossil between fish and tetrapods

name: Dunkleosteus Jr.
description: Unlike her cousin Dunky, Titanichthys was a gentle giant filter feeder — the whale shark of the Devonian. All that armor and she just wanted plankton.
funFacts: An armored fish like Dunkleosteus but a filter feeder
Up to 5 meters long
Had no sharp bony blades — fed on small organisms

name: Cooksonia
description: Not an animal — one of the first land plants! Cooksonia is a tiny fork-shaped pioneer who made dry land livable for everyone else. The ultimate support character.
funFacts: One of the earliest known land plants
Only about 6 cm tall
Made colonization of land possible for animals

name: Aegirocassis
description: A giant filter-feeding anomalocarid. Aegirocassis swam through Ordovician seas like a prehistoric whale shark, peacefully filtering plankton with modified appendages.
funFacts: A gentle giant filter feeder
Related to the predatory Anomalocaris
Had paired flaps for swimming

name: Lystrosaurus
description: The ultimate survivor. After the Great Dying wiped out 96% of all species, Lystrosaurus was one of the few left standing. She once made up 95% of all land vertebrates.
funFacts: Survived the worst mass extinction in history
At one point 95% of all land vertebrates were Lystrosaurus
Found on every continent including Antarctica

name: Ambulocetus
description: The 'walking whale.' Ambulocetus is the missing link between land mammals and whales — she walked AND swam, ambushing prey like a crocodile.
funFacts: Name means 'walking whale'
Could walk on land and swim
Key transitional fossil in whale evolution

name: Pakicetus
description: A tiny wolf-like creature that would one day become... a whale? Pakicetus is proof that evolution's plans are wilder than any fiction.
funFacts: Earliest known whale ancestor
Looked like a small dog or wolf
Lived on land near the shore

name: Sivatherium
description: The moose of giraffes. Sivatherium had massive antler-like ossicones and a stocky build. She looked like a giraffe designed by a committee that included moose.
funFacts: Related to giraffes but much stockier
Had large antler-like ossicones
Survived until about 8,000 years ago

name: Deinotherium
description: An elephant with its tusks pointing DOWN from the lower jaw. Deinotherium is what happens when evolution says 'what if we put those the other way round?'
funFacts: Tusks curved downward from the lower jaw
One of the largest land mammals ever
Name means 'terrible beast'

name: Josephoartigasia
description: The largest rodent ever. Josephoartigasia was a capybara the size of a buffalo. If you think modern capybaras are chill, imagine this absolute unit.
funFacts: Largest rodent ever known
About the size of a buffalo
Related to modern guinea pigs and capybaras

name: Arctodus
description: The short-faced bear — taller than a grizzly and built for speed. Arctodus could outrun a horse and stood 3.4 meters on hind legs. Peak bear performance.
funFacts: Could stand 3.4 meters tall on hind legs
Built for speed unlike modern bears
One of the largest land carnivores in ice age North America

name: Procoptodon
description: A kangaroo the size of a gorilla with a flat face. Procoptodon couldn't hop like modern roos — she walked upright like a short, furry, confused person.
funFacts: Largest kangaroo ever
Could not hop — walked instead
Had a shortened flat face unlike modern kangaroos

name: Megalania
description: A 5-meter venomous monitor lizard. Megalania was basically a Komodo dragon supersized. She ruled prehistoric Australia with terrifying efficiency.
funFacts: Largest venomous animal ever
Related to Komodo dragons
Coexisted with early Aboriginal Australians
